title: D² context interface specification
description: Schema for IAuthContext.spec.json and IRequestContext.spec.json — the source-of-truth shape that DcsvIo.D2.Context.SourceGen reads to emit interfaces + MutableRequestContext.

name.description: Interface name. Conventionally starts with 'I' (e.g. IAuthContext).

extends.description: Fully-qualified base interface name (e.g. DcsvIo.D2.AuthContext.Abstractions.IAuthContext) or null.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.

type.description: Closed type vocabulary recognized by the generator. New types require schema + emitter updates in lockstep.

claim.description: JWT claim name this property maps to (used by FromClaims / FromJwtPayload factories).

trinaryAuth.description: True for `bool?` properties whose null vs false distinction is meaningful (pre-auth vs confirmed-not). Defaults the property to null instead of falling back to default(bool?).

derived.description: Marks this property as computed from other properties at read-time. The named rule is implemented by the generator's MutableEmitter.

default.description: Default value expression (C# literal). Examples: '[]' for empty collections, 'null' for explicit null.

doc.description: XML doc <summary> text rendered on the generated property.

propagate.description: When true, this property is included in the codegen-emitted PropagatedContext record (the cross-hop subset that ships in the x-d2-context AMQP / gRPC / HTTP header). Identity fields (UserId / OrgId / Scopes / ActorChain) MUST NOT be propagated — they rebuild from the JWT at every sync hop.

maxLength.description: Wire-level per-field cap, enforced by the codegen-emitted PropagatedContextSerializer.TryDecode. A forged x-d2-context header with any propagatable field exceeding its cap is dropped wholesale (propagation is opportunistic, never required). On a string-typed field this is the max character length; on a propagated list-of-records field (e.g. CallPath) it is reinterpreted as the max entry count (the call-path depth bound). Only meaningful when propagate=true.

entryIdMaxLength.description: Per-entry id length cap for a propagated list-of-records field (e.g. CallPath). Bounds a single forged entry id so it cannot bloat log scope keys / audit columns even when the entry count is within maxLength. Single source of the cap: both the .NET and TypeScript PropagatedContextSerializer.TryDecode derive it from this value (neither hard-codes the number). Only meaningful on a propagated list-of-records field.

redact.description: When true, this property is PII-bearing and must be redacted from logs / telemetry. Codegen emits a `<TypeName>RedactPaths` constant alongside the interface, listing every redact-marked property as a Pino-redact path. The .NET side consumes the matching `[RedactData]` attribute placement; the cross-spec parity gate (RedactDataVsSpecRedactConsistencyTests) asserts the two sides agree.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
